channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Fine-tuned 7B LLM as a broke student. And Can't even use it 😭.
item.description: Last week, I introduced Monika, a discord bot. As a self-taught student running on an absolute zero budget, this project was less about writing code and much more about hitting hard architectural...

item.title: scan() Is Reduce For Infinite Data
item.description: If you've used RxJS for any meaningful amount of time, you've probably encountered scan(). Maybe you've seen code like this: interval(1000) .pipe( scan( (count) => count + 1, 0 ) ) And maybe...

item.title: Why Coding Stays in Human-AI Collaboration: A Paradox in Stanford's 51 Deployments
item.description: "We rolled out AI and saw no results" and "AI made our development dramatically faster" are being said in the same year, often inside the same company. Where does that gap come from? Stanford...

item.title: 7 Infra Improvement Strategies to Prevent Next.js Deployment Build Failures in 2026
item.description: 7 Infra Improvement Strategies to Prevent Next.js Deployment Build Failures in 2026 Recently, our team's deployment pipeline started showing serious instability. Specifically, we encountered...

item.title: I built a data-contract validator in pure Python (no pandas, no PyYAML) and it caught a 30% revenue ghost
item.description: A few months ago I spent the better part of a day chasing a bug that turned out not to be a bug at all. A downstream dashboard showed revenue had jumped 30% overnight. No deploys, no schema...

item.title: My Claude Code hook silently ate every Korean character, and it took me an hour to figure out why
item.description: I spent an hour last week debugging a hook that wasn't broken. Here's the setup: I run Claude Code on Windows, and I'd written a little UserPromptSubmit hook in PowerShell — a keyword router that...

item.title: Chrono Shift: Time Weaver - A Time-Bending Platformer Built with AI
item.description: What I Built I'm thrilled to present Chrono Shift: Time Weaver – a time-bending puzzle platformer that challenges players to manipulate time itself to overcome obstacles and solve environmental...

item.title: AI didn’t invent code drift. It made it harder to ignore.
item.description: I started noticing this while building a real frontend/backend system with AI assistance. The problem was not that the AI could not code. It could. It could solve local problems, patch visible...

item.title: What Is a SERP API and Why Do SEO and AI Teams Need One?
item.description: Search results look simple from the outside. You type a keyword into Google, Bing, or another search engine, and you get a page of links, snippets, ads, maps, news, images, videos, and sometimes...

item.title: Day 26 - HashiCorp Vault & Secrets Management
item.description: Modern applications depend on secrets. Every application requires: Database Passwords API Keys SSH Keys TLS Certificates Cloud Credentials OAuth Tokens Service Account Keys The biggest question...

item.title: More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel
item.description: Back in 2015, the two-person studio Metanet released N++, a brutally hard 2D platformer that was a decade in the making, building off of previous releases dating back to the freeware Flash title...

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI is warping the video game release calendar
item.description: Who's afraid of the next GTA? Based on the last few days of Summer Game Fest, just about everyone. Grand Theft Auto VI hasn't been present at any of the keynote events, but its presence was felt...

item.title: Final Fantasy VII’s remake trilogy will conclude with Revelation
item.description: Square Enix has officially announced the third and final game in its Final Fantasy VII remake trilogy: Final Fantasy VII Revelation. It will release on multiple platforms simultaneously - PC, PS5,...

item.title: Control Resonant is a sequel — and also a starting point
item.description: Chronologically, Control Resonant is a sequel to 2019's Control. But in most other ways, the games aren't directly connected. To developer Remedy, they're more like two sides of the same coin....

item.title: Congress still can’t decide what to do about warrantless surveillance
item.description: The deadline to reauthorize Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is coming up a week from now on June 12th, and legislators seem no closer to reaching a deal. If this sounds...

item.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
item.description: Seller of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X doesn't consider the behavior a vulnerability.

item.title: Summer Game Fest Live 2026: The biggest news, trailers, and announcements
item.description: Geoff Keighley’s annual June showcase for videos games has officially happened, and it was a big one. Across a two hour event — and another hour for Day of the Devs — we got news about the third...

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
item.description: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this...

item.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
item.description: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.

item.title: Gone in 60 minutes
item.description: It should have been the final straw. The new power couple of editorial failure - Bari Weiss and Nick Bilton - had fired legendary 60 Minutes journalist Scott Pelley. Why? Because he dared to...

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based...

item.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
item.description: While the AI fundraising machine keeps breaking its own records, some founders are building in the other direction. Mirror founder Brynn Putnam just raised money for Board, a startup focused on...

item.title: The Sonos Era 100 speaker is down to its lowest price in months
item.description: Whether you’re considering starting a Sonos speaker setup, or adding to an existing group, the Sonos Era 100 is worth picking up. The compact, capable smart speaker is currently marked down to...

item.title: This is your laptop… on AI
item.description: We're now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything....

item.title: Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
item.description: Microsoft’s AI products aren’t selling, and Github’s been plagued with troubles. WIRED spoke with VP Scott Hanselman about whether the company is in catch-up mode.

item.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
item.description: Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET. The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware...

item.title: The token bill comes due: Inside the industry scramble to manage AI’s runaway costs
item.description: "The whole conversation shifted from tokenmaxxing and 'go fast' to 'we need guardrails, how do we control this?'"

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in May 2026

item.title: The ‘together tech’ wave might be the most intriguing startup bet of 2026

item.title: AI Worm
item.description: Researchers have prototyped an AI-powered internet worm. The coolest thing about the prototype is that it carries its own LLM with it, and runs it on computers that have been broken into. This is...
